mods.name: Sodium
mods.reason: Core rendering engine replacement. Foundation of the entire modpack. Replaces vanilla renderer with massively faster alternative.
mods.notes: Absolute must-have. All sodium-ecosystem mods depend on this.

mods.name: Lithium
mods.reason: Optimizes game physics, mob AI, chunk ticking, and many other server/game logic paths. Massive 1% low improvement.
mods.notes: Zero-config, all optimizations safe. Present in all major packs.

mods.name: FerriteCore
mods.reason: Reduces memory usage significantly by deduplicating blockstate data, baked models, and property maps. Directly improves GC pressure and frame pacing.
mods.notes: Present in all three reference packs. Memory reduction helps stability.

mods.name: ImmediatelyFast
mods.reason: Batches HUD/text/map rendering. Reduces draw calls significantly. Measurable FPS uplift especially in GUI-heavy scenes.
mods.notes: Present in all three reference packs. Well maintained, stable.

mods.name: Entity Culling
mods.reason: Skips rendering entities that are not visible (behind walls). Significant FPS gain in entity-heavy areas. Critical for 1% lows.
mods.notes: Present in all packs. Conservative defaults are safe.

mods.name: More Culling
mods.reason: Extends culling to item frames, signs, leaves, beacons, clouds, rain. Reduces unnecessary rendering work.
mods.notes: Complements Entity Culling without overlap.

mods.name: C2ME (Concurrent Chunk Management Engine)
mods.reason: Parallelizes chunk generation and loading. Massively improves chunk loading speed and smoothness. Key contributor to Kernova's 470 FPS benchmark lead.
mods.notes: Alpha release type but extremely mature and widely used. Core differentiator for Kernova. The risk is accepted because benefits are large.

mods.name: ScalableLux
mods.reason: Optimized lighting engine. Reduces lag from light updates during chunk loading and block placement. Improves frame pacing.
mods.notes: Unique to Kernova among reference packs. Likely contributor to the FPS lead. Replaces Starlight's role.

mods.name: BadOptimizations
mods.reason: Collection of small client-side optimizations: lightmap caching, sky color caching, particle/toast manager skips, renderer caching. Low overhead, measurable gain.
mods.notes: Unique to Kernova. Well-structured with safe individual toggle options.

mods.name: ThreadTweak
mods.reason: Optimizes thread priorities for render and game threads. Improves frame pacing by ensuring render thread gets CPU time.
mods.notes: Unique to Kernova. Small but targeted optimization for frame pacing.

mods.name: Krypton
mods.reason: Optimizes Minecraft networking stack. Reduces network-related stutter and improves multiplayer performance.
mods.notes: Zero-config, no user-facing changes. Proven stable across many versions.

mods.name: Dynamic FPS
mods.reason: Reduces FPS/resource usage when game is unfocused or idle. Saves power and reduces system load.
mods.notes: Present in all packs. Quality of life for system resources.

mods.name: Entity View Distance
mods.reason: Allows fine-tuning entity render distance separately from chunk render distance. Can reduce entity rendering load without affecting terrain visibility.
mods.notes: Useful for users who want high terrain render distance but lower entity load.

mods.name: VMP (Very Many Players)
mods.reason: Optimizes entity tracking, chunk sending, and networking for integrated server. Improves single-player chunk loading and reduces stutter from entity tracking.
mods.notes: Beta release but stable in practice. Primarily benefits chunk delivery speed. Unique Kernova advantage.

mods.name: FastQuit
mods.reason: Allows leaving worlds instantly by saving in background. Eliminates the save screen stall. Zero performance cost during gameplay.
mods.notes: Added from FO reference. Extremely lightweight, improves UX without any gameplay impact.

mods.name: Fast IP Ping
mods.reason: Speeds up server list ping resolution. Improves multiplayer server list loading time.
mods.notes: Very lightweight, no gameplay impact.

mods.name: Fabric API
mods.reason: Required dependency for most Fabric mods. Cannot be omitted.
mods.notes: Mandatory infrastructure.

mods.name: Cloth Config API
mods.reason: Configuration library required by More Culling, Dynamic FPS (optional), and other mods.
mods.notes: Dependency for multiple golden mods.

mods.name: Mod Menu
mods.reason: Essential for accessing mod configuration screens in-game. Required for usability of a modpack.
mods.notes: Standard Fabric modpack infrastructure.

mods.name: Reese's Sodium Options
mods.reason: Improves Sodium video settings UI layout. Better usability for configuration.
mods.notes: Present in all packs. Purely UI, no performance impact.

mods.name: Sodium Extra
mods.reason: Adds toggles for animations, particles, fog, entity rendering details. Allows users to fine-tune visual quality vs performance.
mods.notes: Present in all packs. Gives users granular control.

mods.name: Iris Shaders
mods.reason: Shader support compatible with Sodium. Disabled by default so zero performance cost. Users who want shaders expect this.
mods.notes: Shaders disabled by default in config. No FPS impact unless user enables shaders.

mods.name: Language Reload
mods.reason: Makes language switching instant instead of triggering full resource reload. Reduces unnecessary reloads.
mods.notes: Very lightweight. Present in all packs.

mods.name: WI Zoom
mods.reason: Lightweight zoom feature. Expected by OptiFine users. Zero performance cost. Simpler and lighter than alternatives like Zoomify.
mods.notes: Chosen over Zoomify for minimal footprint. Single feature, no bloat.

mods.name: YOSBR (Your Options Shall Be Respected)
mods.reason: Preserves user options across modpack updates. Critical for update workflow.
mods.notes: Ensures modpack updates do not reset user preferences.
